DISCOVERY 302, LAINTIFF POTRERO MEDIA CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS, MONETARY SANCTIONS, AND CONTEMPT ORDER. DISCUSSED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED: MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS AND MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT JONES TO REMIT PAYMENT IN THE AMOUNT OF $4,235.00 TO PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL BY MAY 8, 2011. HOWEVER, COURT STAYED PAYMENT DATE REQUIREMENT UNTIL FURTHER NOTICE. IN ADDITION, THE COURT CONTINUED THE MATTER FOR AN OSC RE CONTEMPT FOR 04/21/11 AT 9:00AM TO SET THE ACTUAL CONTEMPT HEARING (UNLESS DEFENDANT SIGNS GOGGLE AUTHORIZATION). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302)

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF POTRERO MEDIA CORPORATION'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE CONTEMPT. COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING WAS THAT A HEARING WAS REQUIRED UNLESS DEFENDANT JONES HAD SIGNED THE AUTHORIZATION/RELEASE AS ORDERED BY THE COURT. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL STATED TO THE COURT THAT MR. JONES DID NOT SIGN THE DOCUMENT. MR. JONES FAILED TO APPEAR AT THIS HEARING. THE COURT THEREFORE WILL ISSUE A BENCH WARRANT FOR MR. JONES AND SET BAIL AT $10,000.00. IN ADDITION, THE COURT SET 05/31/11 AS THE PAYMENT DUE DATE FOR THE SANCTIONS FROM THE PRIOR HEARING. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER ON THE SANCTIONS. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; NOT REPORTED

ORDER RE: DEFENDANTS THE LEAD CONSORTIUM, LLC & RYAN OUSDAHL'S OBJECTION TO THE DECLARATION OF KYLE MCBRIEN IN SUPPORT OF APPLICATION FOR TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER

LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED IN PART. PLAINTIFF HAS SHOWN THAT THE BALANCE OF EQUITIES WEIGHS IN ITS FAVOR AND A LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS FOR A CLAIM FOR MISAPPROPRIATION OF TRADE SECRETS. THE COURT WOULD STRIKE "E" FROM THE PROPOSED PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION THAT RESTRAINS DEFENDANTS FROM "DISPARAGING PMC OR ITS OFFICERS OR EMPLOYEES." BOND SHALL BE POSTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $10,000 DUE TO FAILURE OF DEFENDANTS TO MORE ADEQUATELY ADDRESS THE ISSUE OF DAMAGES. FURTHER, COUNSEL ARE TO COOPERATE REGARDING CLIENT LIST (EXAMPLES: HIRE A NEUTRAL OR SUBMIT INFORMATION TO COUNSEL "FOR COUNSEL'S EYES ONLY"). Nicholas Carlin, & Cari COhorn, Esqs.appeared for plaintiff(s) Potrero Media Corporation,
Default has been entered against the Defendant(s).
Plaintiff's witness Carol Nash-Witt, sworn and examined.
After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff(s), and against the Defaulted Defendant(s) Matthew Jones for Judgment as follows:

Treble Damages $847,809.00
Prejudgment Interest - $19,782.36
Attorney's Fees - $225,695.50
Costs - $6,132.00
Prior Sanctions - $7,235.00
Total judgment in the amount of $1,106,654.86

Counsel to prepare Judgment and submit to Dept. 514 for signature.

APPEARANCES: Nicholas A. Carlin, Esq. & Cari A. Cohorn, Esq. of Phillips, Erlewine & Given LLP 398-0900 for pltf & moving party; no other appearances.

RULING: Court's tentative ruling was that a hearing was required unless defendant Jones had signed the authorization/release as ordered by the Court. Plaintiff's counsel stated to the Court that Mr. Jones did not sign the document. Mr. Jones failed to appear at this hearing. The Court therefore will issue a bench warrant for Mr. Jones and set bail at $10,000.00. In addition, the Court set 05/31/11 as the payment due date for the sanctions from the prior hearing. Plaintiff to submit a proposed order on the sanctions. Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-10-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT POTRERO MEDIA CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT OF DEFENDANTS/CROSS-COMPLAINANTS THE LEAD CONSORTIUM, LLC, AND RYAN OUSDAHL.

APPEARANCES: Cari Cohorn, Esq. of Phillips, Erlewine & Given LLP 398-0900 for pltf/x-deft & moving party; Scott A. Kron, Esq. of Kron & Card 949/367-0520 for defts/x-complainants & opposing parties The Lead Consortium, LLC & Ryan Ousdahl (appearing via CourtCall).

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. AS TO 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION, DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST CLARIFY WHAT THE TERMS OF THE CONTRACT ARE AND THE RELATIONSHIP OF THOSE TERMS TO THE ALLEGED BREACH. AS TO THE 2ND, 5TH AND 6TH CAUSES OF ACTION THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. In addition, the Court overruled the demurrer as to the 7th cause of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the 8th cause of action. Plaintiff to prepare a proposed form of order. 10 days leave to amend from entry of order. (Court takes judicial notice of plaintiff's request but refrains from making any determination on demurrer.) (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT POTRERO MEDIA CORPORATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF CROSS-COMPLAINT OF DEFENDANTS/CROSS-COMPLAINANTS THE LEAD CONSORTIUM, LLC, AND RYAN OUSDAHL.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - MOTION TO STRIKE GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES TO THE EXTENT THAT THE REQUEST ARISES UNDER THE 9TH AND 10TH CAUSES OF ACTION. GRANTED AS TO THE ALLEGATIONS REFERRING TO "OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED". DENIED AS TO THE PRAYER FOR ATTORNEYS FEE'S UNDER CCC 1021.5. Plaintiff to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause To Defendants Re: Temporary Restraining Order
Off Calendar Aug-24-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE TO DEFENDANTS RE: TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER. Appearances: Nicholas A. Carlin of Phillips Erlewine & Given, LLP (398.0900) for plaintiff and moving party; Scott A. Kron (949.337.2762) for defendants and responding parties. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted in part. Plaintiff has shown that the balance of equities weighs in its favor and a likelihood of success on the merits for a claim for misappropriation of trade secrets. The Court would strike "e" from the proposed preliminary injunction that restrains defendants from "disparaging PMC or its officers or employees." Bond shall be posted in the amount of $10,000 due to failure of defendants to more adequately address the issue of damages. Further, counsel are to cooperate regarding client list (examples: hire a neutral or submit information to counsel "for counsel's eyes only"). Orders re: preliminary injunction and defendants' objections signed in open court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
